vulnerability.reason: Reference describes recommendation algorithms' engagement metrics being easily gamed by clickbait; generated shifts sensitivity to content creators' dependency on engagement-optimized curation with no alternative, missing the algorithmic vulnerability.
vulnerability.reference: Recommendation algorithms optimize for engagement metrics easily gamed by clickbait and sensationalism.
vulnerability.generated: Content creators’ income and audiences’ attention depend on engagement-optimized algorithmic curation, offering no alternative to resist score-chasing.

impact.reason: Both affected party (user experience) and damage type (degradation) are present; generated also adds trust erosion and platform losses, but core impact is covered.
impact.reference: User experience degrades as low-quality clickbait crowds out authentic content.
impact.generated: Pervasive inauthentic content degrades user experience, reduces trust, and erodes platform user base and revenue.

dose_response.reason: Same causal arc: classifier-induced feature markets incentivize engagement/score-chasing modifications, leading to inauthentic/low-quality content that degrades user experience and erodes trust; generated adds platform revenue erosion but preserves the mechanism.
dose_response.reference: Classifier-induced feature markets incentivize engagement-optimizing modifications, exposing content creators and consumers to low-quality clickbait, leading to degraded user experience and erosion of trust in information sources.
dose_response.generated: Co-optimized learning and pricing in classifier-induced feature markets incentivize score-chasing modifications, which, on social media platforms, leave creators with no alternative but to invest in engagement-optimized curation, driving inauthentic content that degrades user experience and erodes platform trust and revenue.
